message:
feat(observability,vfs): split unix-fallback counter by read vs write path

Previously the io_uring VfsFile recorded a single
unix_fallbacks_total counter when a submission flow bailed to a blocking
libc read/write. Operators couldn't tell whether the fallback pressure
was skewed toward reads (typically a cold-cache scan) or writes (a
ring-full or submission-rejected condition), which matters for sizing.

Changes:
- fsqlite-observability/src/lib.rs:
  * IoUringLatencySnapshot gains read_unix_fallbacks_total,
    write_unix_fallbacks_total
  * IoUringLatencyMetrics gains the same two AtomicU64 counters
  * New record_read_unix_fallback / record_write_unix_fallback methods
    each bump the direction-specific counter and then call the
    existing record_unix_fallback() so the aggregate counter is still
    accurate for callers that don't care about direction.

- fsqlite-vfs/src/uring.rs:
  * Import the new record_io_uring_read_unix_fallback and
    record_io_uring_write_unix_fallback; drop the bare
    record_io_uring_unix_fallback import.
  * Read path fallback call site → record_io_uring_read_unix_fallback()
  * Write path fallback call site → record_io_uring_write_unix_fallback()
  * Tests updated: the availability assertion now looks at the
    direction-specific counter; the disabled-runtime assertion adds two
    new expectations that both direction-specific counters advanced.

Backward compatible with any existing metric consumer: the aggregate
unix_fallbacks_total is still emitted and advances on every fallback.
